entities.name: Discipline in Restraint
entities.context: The ability to stay below maximum capacity; more critical for sustainable conditioning than maximum effort discipline

entities.name: Local Optimum Trap
entities.context: Working at 100% capacity in the present prevents structural changes needed to operate at higher levels in the future

entities.name: Sustainable Conditioning
entities.context: Long-term physiological growth achieved through managed effort rather than over-exertion

entities.name: Stress-Recovery Windows
entities.context: Specific biological timeframes required for systems to adapt; pushing limits triggers survival responses instead of conditioning responses

entities.name: Intensity Caps
entities.context: Explicit effort limits in training or development programs to ensure physiological or mental buffer for recovery

relationships.context: Restraint is more critical for sustainable conditioning than maximum effort
Lack of restraint exemplifies the Local Optimum trap - short-term maximization prevents long-term growth
Biological basis for why managed effort enables adaptation
Practical implementation of restraint discipline in training programs
